notes: LoadOut-original second-focal-plane hunting duplex: four heavy outer posts taper to a thin center crosshair. Subtensions are valid only at the spec magnification (9x for the canonical mid-tube hunting variable). LoadOut original art; calibrated for interoperability with the classic public-domain duplex pattern.

subtensionsOriginal.notes: First-principles geometry: thin inner crosshair extends 7 MOA each direction at 9x, thick outer posts span 7-18 MOA at 0.55 MOA bar width. Derived from typical 1-inch tube SFP duplex proportions without reference to any vendor's published spec.

subtensionsCalibrated.notes: Subtensions tuned to the published Leupold Duplex spec at 9x: 0.75 MOA thick-post width, ~7.5 MOA from center to inner thick-post edge. A 1-inch wide reference at 9x subtends ~9.55 MOA between thin-crosshair endpoint and the inner thick-post edge, supporting the classic Leupold-style 1-inch-at-100-yd ranging shortcut.

calibrationProvenance.verifiedBy: LoadOut research, citing Leupold's published VX-3HD product page and the long-standing public-domain four-quadrant duplex pattern.
calibrationProvenance.notes: The four-quadrant duplex pattern itself is public-domain reticle prior art going back decades; the LoadOut variant is original art with subtensions tuned for interoperability with the dominant 1-inch-tube SFP hunting duplex. The 'Duplex' word is a Leupold trademark in optics — never used in any user-visible LoadOut string per CLAUDE.md section 30.

notes: LoadOut-original second-focal-plane four-dot BDC for hunting cartridges: thin inner crosshair, heavy outer posts, four numbered holdover dots labeled 200 / 300 / 400 / 500 yd. Subtensions are valid only at the spec magnification (10x) with the calibrated zero set at 100 yd. Calibrated for interoperability with typical hunting BDC layouts.

subtensionsOriginal.notes: First-principles drop chart for a generic 2700 fps mid-weight hunting load (G1 BC ~0.4) with a 100-yard zero, computed from a standard ICAO atmosphere ballistic table. Vertical positions shown in MOA at 10x; render unit is BDC so the layout is anchored to range labels rather than to the angular grid.

subtensionsCalibrated.calibratedCartridgeFamily: Generic mid-weight hunting trajectory: .308 Win 150 gr, .30-06 165 gr, 7mm Rem Mag 150 gr, .270 Win 130 gr
subtensionsCalibrated.notes: Subtensions tuned to match the published Burris Ballistic Plex E1 holdover spacing at 10x: holds align with the typical hunting cartridge envelope at 200 / 300 / 400 / 500 yd from a 100-yard zero. The published curve is an envelope, not a single-cartridge calibration; users with non-conforming loads should verify in Find By My Scope before use.

calibrationProvenance.calibratedAgainst: Burris Ballistic Plex E1 SFP (Fullfield IV mid-magnification spec)
calibrationProvenance.verifiedBy: LoadOut research, citing Burris's published Fullfield IV product page and the Ballistic Plex E1 holdover envelope chart.
calibrationProvenance.notes: The four-dot BDC pattern is a widely-used hunting reticle convention; the LoadOut variant is original art with hold spacings tuned for interoperability with the published Burris Ballistic Plex E1 envelope. 'Ballistic Plex' is a Burris trademark in optics — never used in any user-visible LoadOut string per CLAUDE.md section 30.

notes: LoadOut-original second-focal-plane heavy-crosshair reticle: thick left, right, and lower posts with a thin upper crosshair. Classic European-style fast-acquisition pattern derived from the long-standing public-domain three-post layout. Subtensions are valid only at the spec magnification (10x).

subtensionsOriginal.notes: First-principles geometry: thin upper crosshair extends 9 MOA, three heavy posts (left, right, lower) span 4.5-18 MOA at 0.5 MOA bar width. Derived from typical European 30 mm-tube SFP three-post proportions without reference to any vendor's published spec.

subtensionsCalibrated.notes: Subtensions tuned to match the Schmidt and Bender L7 published spec at 10x: thick post bar width ~0.55 MOA, post inner edge ~3.5 MOA from the optical center, lower post extends from ~7 MOA to ~17.5 MOA below center. The German-style heavy crosshair is intended for low-light fast acquisition; the thin upper crosshair preserves precision aiming when needed.

calibrationProvenance.calibratedAgainst: Schmidt and Bender L7 SFP (Zenith 1.1-8x24 mid-magnification spec)
calibrationProvenance.verifiedBy: LoadOut research, citing Schmidt and Bender's published Zenith 1.1-8x24 product page and the long-standing public-domain three-post heavy-crosshair pattern.
calibrationProvenance.notes: The three-post heavy-crosshair pattern is public-domain reticle prior art used by every major European optics maker for over a century; the LoadOut variant is original art with subtensions tuned for interoperability with the dominant 30 mm-tube SFP heavy-crosshair layout. The 'L7' designation is a Schmidt and Bender catalog ID — never used in any user-visible LoadOut string per CLAUDE.md section 30.

notes: LoadOut-original second-focal-plane LPVO chevron BDC for 1-6x scopes: inverted-V chevron with a daylight-bright dot at the chevron tip, four numbered BDC dots below for 5.56 NATO holds at 400 / 500 / 600 / 800 m. Subtensions are valid only at the spec magnification (6x). The chevron-with-BDC pattern is a longstanding combat-optic convention; LoadOut variant is original art.

subtensionsOriginal.notes: First-principles drop chart for 5.56 NATO M193 / M855 from a 14.5-inch barrel with a 100-yard zero, computed from a standard ICAO atmosphere ballistic table. Vertical positions shown in MOA at 6x; render unit is BDC so the layout is anchored to range labels rather than to the angular grid.

subtensionsCalibrated.calibratedCartridgeFamily: 5.56 NATO M193 / M855 from a 14.5-inch to 20-inch barrel, 100-yard zero
subtensionsCalibrated.notes: Subtensions tuned to match the published Trijicon ACOG TA31 chevron BDC spec proportionally rescaled for an LPVO at 6x: chevron base ~1.85 MOA wide, holds at 4 / 5 / 6 / 8 (hundreds of meters). The TA31 is a 4x fixed prism optic; the LoadOut LPVO variant preserves the same hold spacing at 6x by scaling proportionally. Hold spacing is a 5.56 NATO ballistics envelope; users with non-conforming loads or barrel lengths should verify in Find By My Scope.

calibrationProvenance.calibratedAgainst: Trijicon ACOG TA31 chevron BDC (4x fixed prism, 5.56 NATO calibration)
calibrationProvenance.verifiedBy: LoadOut research, citing Trijicon's published TA31 product page. The chevron-with-BDC pattern's foundational patents have largely expired and the design is now broadly used across the combat-optic ecosystem.
calibrationProvenance.notes: The chevron BDC pattern is widely used across the LPVO and prism-optic ecosystem since its foundational patents lapsed; the LoadOut variant is original art with hold spacings tuned for interoperability with the dominant 5.56 NATO calibration. 'ACOG' is a Trijicon trademark in optics — never used in any user-visible LoadOut string per CLAUDE.md section 30.
